title: AM Best Assigns Issue Credit Rating to MetLife, Inc.'s New Senior Unsecured Notes
text: OLDWICK, N.J.--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- #insurance--AM Best has assigned a Long-Term Issue Credit Rating of “a-” (Excellent) to the newly issued $1 billion, 5.375% senior unsecured notes due July 15, 2033, issued by MetLife, Inc. (MetLife) (headquartered in New York, NY) [NYSE: MET]. The outlook assigned to this Credit Rating (rating) is stable. All other ratings of MetLife and its subsidiaries remain unchanged. The proceeds from this debt issuance are expected to be used for general corporate purposes, which may.

title: MetLife: An Investment That Ensures Portfolios
text: MetLife's share price has slipped this year, but the company raised its dividend and is focusing on M&A activity, making it a potential opportunity for investors in an improving economy. The company is expected to focus on paying down debt, completing its share repurchase plan, and expanding M&A activity rather than significantly increasing its dividend in the future. Despite risks such as debt, high Beta, a Hold Quant Rating, and struggling cash flow, MetLife is seen as a financially healthy company and a potential opportunity for long-term investors.
